check.command: show running-config | section ^callhome | include "^ enable$"

checks.description: The Cisco switch must not be configured to have any feature enabled that calls home to the vendor.

check.command: show running-config | include "deny icmp .* fragments"

checks.description: The Cisco switch must be configured to drop all fragmented Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) packets destined to itself.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ no ip arp gratuitous request$"

checks.description: The Cisco switch must be configured to have Gratuitous ARP disabled on all external interfaces.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ ip directed-broadcast$"

checks.description: The Cisco switch must be configured to have IP directed broadcast disabled on all interfaces.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ ip unreachables$"

checks.description: The Cisco switch must be configured to have Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) unreachable messages disabled on all external interfaces.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ ip redirects$"

checks.description: The Cisco switch must be configured to have Internet Control Message Protocol (ICMP) redirect messages disabled on all external interfaces.

check.command: show running-config | awk 'BEGIN{bad=0} /^[[:space:]]*[0-9]*[[:space:]]*deny[[:space:]]/ && $0 !~ /[[:space:]]log([[:space:]]|$)/ {bad=1} END{if(!bad) print "Compliant"}'

checks.description: The Cisco switch must be configured to log all packets that have been dropped at interfaces via an ACL.

check.command: show running-config | sh -c 'cat > /tmp/acl.$$; grep -Fq "deny ip 0.0.0.0/8 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 10.0.0.0/8 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 100.64.0.0/10 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 127.0.0.0/8 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 169.254.0.0/16 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 172.16.0.0/12 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 192.0.0.0/24 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 192.0.2.0/24 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 192.168.0.0/16 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 198.18.0.0/15 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 198.51.100.0/24 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 203.0.113.0/24 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && grep -Fq "deny ip 224.0.0.0/3 any log" /tmp/acl.$$ && printf Compliant; rm -f /tmp/acl.$$'

checks.description: The Cisco perimeter switch must be configured to block inbound packets with source Bogon IP address prefixes.

check.command: show running-config | include ^no ip source-route$

checks.description: The Cisco perimeter switch must be configured to block all packets with any IP options.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ no lldp transmit$"

checks.description: The Cisco perimeter switch must be configured to have Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) disabled on all external interfaces.

check.command: show running-config | include "^no cdp enable$"

checks.description: The Cisco perimeter switch must be configured to have Cisco Discovery Protocol (CDP) disabled on all external interfaces.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ ip proxy-arp$"

checks.description: The Cisco perimeter switch must be configured to have Proxy ARP disabled on all external interfaces.

check.command: show running-config | sh -c 'cat > /tmp/nxmgmt.$$; grep -Fq "deny tcp any any eq tacacs log" /tmp/nxmgmt.$$ && grep -Fq "deny tcp any any eq 22 log" /tmp/nxmgmt.$$ && grep -Fq "deny udp any any eq snmp log" /tmp/nxmgmt.$$ && grep -Fq "deny udp any any eq snmptrap log" /tmp/nxmgmt.$$ && grep -Fq "deny udp any any eq syslog log" /tmp/nxmgmt.$$ && printf Compliant; rm -f /tmp/nxmgmt.$$'

checks.description: The Cisco perimeter switch must be configured to block all outbound management traffic.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ disable-connected-check$"

checks.description: The Cisco BGP switch must be configured to check whether a single-hop eBGP peer is directly connected.

check.command: show running-config | section "^router bgp" | include "^ no enforce-first-as$"

checks.description: The Cisco BGP switch must be configured to reject route advertisements from BGP peers that do not list their autonomous system (AS) number as the first AS in the AS_PATH attribute.

check.command: show running-config | section "^router bgp" | include "^ maximum-prefix "

checks.description: The Cisco BGP switch must be configured to use the maximum prefixes feature to protect against route table flooding and prefix de-aggregation attacks.

check.command: show running-config | section "^router bgp" | include "update-source (loopback|lo)[0-9]*$"

checks.description: The Cisco BGP switch must be configured to use its loopback address as the source address for iBGP peering sessions.

check.command: show running-config | section "^mpls ldp configuration" | include "^ router-id (loopback|lo)[0-9]*$"

checks.description: The Cisco MPLS switch must be configured to use its loopback address as the source address for LDP peering sessions.

check.command: show running-config | section "^router (ospf|isis)" | include "^ mpls ldp sync$"

checks.description: The Cisco MPLS switch must be configured to synchronize Interior Gateway Protocol (IGP) and LDP to minimize packet loss when an IGP adjacency is established prior to LDP peers completing label exchange.

check.command: show running-config | awk 'BEGIN{te=0;rr=0} /mpls[[:space:]]+traffic-eng[[:space:]]+tunnels/{te=1} /signaling[[:space:]]+refresh[[:space:]]+reduction/{rr=1} END{if(!te || rr) print "Compliant"}'

checks.description: The MPLS switch with RSVP-TE enabled must be configured with message pacing to adjust maximum burst and maximum number of RSVP messages to an output queue based on the link speed and input queue size of adjacent core switches.

check.command: show running-config | include "^no mpls ip propagate-ttl$"

checks.description: The Cisco MPLS switch must be configured to have TTL Propagation disabled.

check.command: show running-config | section "^interface " | include "^ storm-control broadcast level "

checks.description: The Cisco PE switch providing Virtual Private LAN Services (VPLS) must be configured to have traffic storm control thresholds on CE-facing interfaces.

check.command: show running-config | include "^no ip igmp snooping$|^ no ip igmp snooping$"

checks.description: The Cisco PE switch must be configured to implement Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) or Multicast Listener Discovery (MLD) snooping for each Virtual Private LAN Services (VPLS) bridge domain.

check.command: show running-config | section "^bridge-domain " | include "^ mac limit maximum addresses "

checks.description: The Cisco PE switch must be configured to limit the number of MAC addresses it can learn for each Virtual Private LAN Services (VPLS) bridge domain.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ ip verify unicast source reachable-via any$"

checks.description: The Cisco PE switch must be configured with Unicast Reverse Path Forwarding (uRPF) loose mode enabled on all CE-facing interfaces.

expected.substring: ip verify unicast source reachable-via any

check.command: show running-config | include ^no ip source-route$

checks.description: The Cisco PE switch must be configured to ignore or drop all packets with any IP options.

check.command: show running-config | awk 'BEGIN{c=0;d=0;p=0;b=0} /^class-map match-all SCAVENGER$/{c=1} /^ match ip dscp cs1$/{d=1} /^ class SCAVENGER$/{p=1} /^  bandwidth percent 5$/{b=1} END{if(c&&d&&p&&b) print "Compliant"}'

checks.description: The Cisco switch must be configured to enforce a Quality-of-Service (QoS) policy to limit the effects of packet flooding denial-of-service (DoS) attacks.

check.command: show running-config | awk 'BEGIN{border=0;deny=0} /ip pim border/{border=1} /deny ip any 239\.0\.0\.0\/8/{deny=1} END{if(border&&deny) print "Compliant"}'

checks.description: The Cisco multicast edge switch must be configured to establish boundaries for administratively scoped multicast traffic.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ ip igmp state-limit "

checks.description: The Cisco multicast Designated switch (DR) must be configured to limit the number of mroute states resulting from Internet Group Management Protocol (IGMP) and Multicast Listener Discovery (MLD) Host Membership Reports.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ip pim spt-threshold infinity "

checks.description: The Cisco multicast Designated switch (DR) must be configured to set the shortest-path tree (SPT) threshold to infinity to minimalize source-group (S, G) state within the multicast topology where Any Source Multicast (ASM) is deployed.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ip msdp password "

checks.description: The Cisco Multicast Source Discovery Protocol (MSDP) switch must be configured to authenticate all received MSDP packets.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ip msdp sa-limit "

checks.description: The Cisco Multicast Source Discovery Protocol (MSDP) switch must be configured to limit the amount of source-active messages it accepts on a per-peer basis.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ip msdp peer .* connect-source "

checks.description: The Cisco Multicast Source Discovery Protocol (MSDP) switch must be configured to use a loopback address as the source address when originating MSDP traffic.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ ipv6 nd hop-limit 32$"

checks.description: The Cisco switch must be configured to advertise a hop limit of at least 32 in Switch Advertisement messages for IPv6 stateless auto-configuration deployments.

check.command: show running-config | include "[Ff][Ee][Cc][0-9AaBbCcDdEeFf]*:"

checks.description: The Cisco switch must not be configured to use IPv6 Site Local Unicast addresses.

check.command: show running-config | include "^ ipv6 nd suppress-ra$"

checks.description: The Cisco perimeter switch must be configured to suppress Router Advertisements on all external IPv6-enabled interfaces.
